About

Who I am.

Not the kind of person who plans five years out. I get interested in things, go deep, and build to understand. That's led me into UI/UX, AI systems, and a few programming languages I've grown comfortable with.

Design Side

I'm drawn to interfaces that don't make you think — the kind where everything is in the right place before you realize it should be. I study how information is organized, how spacing creates rhythm, and why some designs feel calm while others feel chaotic.

AI Side

I run language models locally, try different prompting strategies, and work to understand what's actually happening under the hood. Less "using AI" and more "trying to understand AI" — there's a meaningful difference between the two.

Quick Facts

  • → Logical problem solver — numbers and systems make sense to me
  • → Comfortable switching between design thinking and code
  • → Perfect score in computer science annual exam
  • → Solves 3×3 Rubik's cube under a minute
  • → Consistent — shows up and does the work
Currently Exploring
◈

Motion Systems

How animation can carry meaning rather than just decoration. The difference between motion that guides and motion that distracts.

◉

Model Behavior

How LLMs respond to different context structures, temperature settings, and system prompt framing. Less about outputs, more about patterns.

◎

Design at Scale

What breaks when a design system grows. Token governance, component versioning, and why consistent spacing is deceptively hard.

Contact Me →

Designing Interfaces. Exploring AI. Building Ideas.
